rationale: The citing paper explicitly frames its contribution as extending prior conjoint-design work on range/number-of-level biases and discriminability, and the cited 2008 paper is directly about a mechanism for the number-of-levels effect in conjoint (attentional contrast shaping derived importance). Given this close construct and mechanism overlap (conjoint importance-weight bias from level representation), this is plausibly a substantive theoretical/method source rather than an incidental mention.
